patch.value:
## Context

Semantic/hybrid search required an embedding service (Ollama/OpenAI) + vector store, leaving air-gapped/CI/zero-setup users on the naive field-weighted keyword scorer. There was also no offline way to MEASURE retrieval quality, so relevance regressions went undetected.

## Decision

1. Ship a built-in **BM25** lexical ranker (Okapi BM25: IDF + k1 saturation + b length-normalization) that runs entirely in-process over the existing search corpus. It activates for --semantic/--hybrid when search.provider=bm25 (always) or auto (offline fallback only when no embedding/extension provider). Plain keyword mode is unchanged.
2. Ship **pm eval**: an nDCG@k / MRR@k / precision@k / recall@k runner over a git-tracked golden-query set, with --fail-under as a CI gate.

## Rationale

- BM25 is a strong, dependency-free lexical baseline — far better than raw term counts, available with zero setup, and reuses the configurable search.corpus_fields. It is honestly tagged matched_fields:[bm25] and documented as below dense retrieval.
- Reusing combineHybridHits (with a dense-component label param) and forceExactIdHitsToTop kept BM25 consistent with the vector path (exact-ID guarantee preserved) and minimized new branches.
- A binary-relevance golden set is cheap to curate yet supports DCG's positional discount; --fail-under makes relevance a build-gateable signal. The eval harness is the validation vehicle for BM25 and any future provider/weight change.

## Alternatives considered

- Bundling a WASM embedding model for true offline dense retrieval: heavy artifact, deferred. BM25 is the pragmatic first step (matches pm-75k9 acceptance).
- A new blocking CI workflow job: deferred in favor of the reusable --fail-under primitive (CI can adopt it without a curated corpus dependency in core CI).

## Consequences

search.provider now recognizes bm25/auto; search.bm25.{k1,b} are validated/persisted knobs; eval-queries.json lives at <pmRoot>/search/ (gitignore negation keeps the cache dir ignored). pm eval is a core command (contracts + help + fixture).
ADR: offline BM25 lexical provider + relevance eval harness for search quality
